title: Math (cont.) + System design framework + interview-prep canon
focus: Happy Number / Plus One + the reusable design scaffold + the canonical interview repos.

items.label: Read: Chip Huyen — ML systems design TOC
interviewQuestions: Walk me through your design framework on a fresh prompt.
What goes wrong if you skip the metrics step?
How do you decide between batch vs realtime inference?

items.label: Patrick Halina — ML Systems Design Interview Guide
interviewQuestions: What's the structure Patrick Halina recommends for the design interview?
Where do most candidates get stuck in design interviews — and why?
How do you handle the 'I don't know' moment in a design interview?

items.label: Memorize the scaffold: clarify → metrics → data → model → serving → monitoring → trade-off close
interviewQuestions: Recite the scaffold cold: clarify → metrics → data → model → serving → monitoring → trade-off close.
Why does the trade-off close matter as much as the design itself?
What's the one trade-off you'd defend hardest if pushed?

items.label: khangich/machine-learning-interview — full prep guide
interviewQuestions: What's the structure khangich recommends for an ML interview prep schedule?
Which sections of the repo would you prioritize for a senior MLE loop?
How would you adapt the repo for an ML Architect loop?

items.label: alirezadir/Machine-Learning-Interviews — companion repo
interviewQuestions: What does alirezadir's repo cover that khangich's doesn't?
Walk me through one design pattern from the repo you'd use in an interview.
How do you avoid memorizing answers from these repos?

items.label: Chip Huyen — ML Interviews Book (free)
interviewQuestions: What's the most useful chapter in Chip Huyen's ML Interviews Book for design rounds?
How would you use the open-ended questions in the book for self-practice?
What did you learn from the book that surprised you?
Walk me through your design framework on a fresh prompt.
What goes wrong if you skip the metrics step?
How do you decide between batch vs realtime inference?
